nameMeanings.name: Brusee
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Dutch Brizee: altered form of French Brisé, which is probably from an old vernacular form of the personal name Brictius (see Brice ) and, in part, a habitational name from Brisé, a place in Loire-Atlantique. Compare Brazie and Brazee .

variants.name: Brazie
meanings: Altered form of   Bresee  , a surname of Dutch origin.

variants.name: Brice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Antoine, Cecile, Chantal, Chantale, Damien, Donat, Franck, Ghislaine, Jacques, Jean Claude, Jean Francois.

French, English, and Scottish: from the Old French personal name Brice, probably Celtic (Gaulish) in origin but not firmly explained (Latinized as Bri(c)tius, Bric(c)ius, or Brixius), which was borne by a 5th-century Christian saint who succeeded Saint Martin as bishop of Tours. Consequently, it became a popular personal name in France and Germany in the early Middle Ages. It was imported to England and Scotland by the Normans. Compare German Brixius .

variants.name: Brazee
meanings: Altered form of   Bresee  , a surname of Dutch origin.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: Robert Brusee II was born from 7 January 1030 to 6 January 1031, in Normandy, France. He had at least 1 son with Agnes Clare. He died from 7 January 1094 to 6 January 1095, in Skelton Castle, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Brusee II(은)는 1030년 1월 7일부터 1031년 1월 6일까지 프랑스 노먼디에서 태어났다. 그는 Agnes Clare(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1094년 1월 7일부터 1095년 1월 6일까지 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 스켈턴 카스틀에서 사망했다.
